summary: The dental pulp is primarily innervated by sensory afferents derived from the maxillary and mandibular branches of the trigeminal nerve (CN V), responsible for detecting pain and temperature changes. Unlike taste sensations involving the facial nerve, pulpal innervation is strictly governed by the trigeminovascular system.
